name: Zhu Ye Qing
terroir: Emei Mountain (~800-1500m), subtropical with heavy rainfall and persistent mist, Buddhist temple tea gardens
cultivar: Local Sichuan cultivar with flat, needle-like buds
flavor_profile: A visually striking Sichuan green tea made entirely from single buds that stand upright in the glass — some floating at the surface, others sinking — creating an elegant 'bamboo forest' effect. The best batches deliver vibrant green fruit notes (apple, mango) with a youthful, verdant zing. Most commercial production emphasizes a clean, fresh, grassy taste. The flat buds are tender and delicate, brewing a light, sweet cup with a crisp finish.

brewing.tips: Best brewed in a tall clear glass to show off the vertical leaf dance. Start with a slightly longer steep to open the flat buds, then transition to grandpa-style for the final infusions.
